title: indigofera wightii
intro: Indigofera wightii, also known as Kutki or Tephrosia, is a plant species belonging to the legume family, commonly found in tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, containing various alkaloids, including kutkiin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties. The health benefits of Indigofera wightii include its ability to support liver function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of jaundice and other liver-related disorders. The plant can be used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, powders, and capsules, which are often used in Ayurvedic medicine to support liver health and overall well-being.
